Title: Elsa breaks new ground with autonomous underwater vehicle job for oil major
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Elsa Bhd has secured a contract to provide autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) subsea structure inspection services for a major exploration and production (E&P) company across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak.
Elsa claimed that this is a first-of-its-kind AUV deployment for subsea structure inspection involving vertical offshore platform structures in Malaysia.
Elsa is an integrated oil and gas services and equipment (OGSE) solutions provider.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group said its subsidiary Elsa Energy Sdn Bhd had accepted Letters of Award for the contract.
It has a firm order value for AUV inspection works commencing in the third quarter of 2026.
The contract runs from June 19 this year to June 18, 2027, with an option to extend for an additional year.
It also provides opportunities for further work orders to be issued throughout the contract tenure.
Under the contract, Elsa Energy will deploy AUV systems to carry out autonomous underwater inspections of offshore platform structures.
The scope of work includes the provision of AUV systems, platform deployed underwater inspections, as well as data collection and analysis.
Elsa said the technology is designed to efficiently inspect vertical offshore platform structures, complementing its existing capabilities in inspecting horizontal subsea pipeline assets using a different AUV technology.
The group said its track record in inspecting both vertical and horizontal subsea assets positions it to offer a comprehensive and fully integrated subsea asset inspection solution.
Elsa managing director Daniel Ilham Khong said the contract is significant because of what it represents for the company's robotics and autonomous inspection business positioning.
"We believe this is a first-of-its-kind AUV deployment for subsea structure inspection involving vertical offshore platform structures in Malaysia.
"Unlike pipeline inspection, this application requires a distinctly different category of autonomous technology to navigate complex vertical architecture," he said in a statement.
Daniel said the contract reflects the accelerating adoption of autonomous underwater inspection technology within Malaysia's offshore oil and gas industry.
The AUV contract aligns closely with Elsa's post-listing growth trajectory, which prioritises robotics-enabled inspection, drone-based applications and advanced asset integrity solutions.
The company said the long-term addressable market for autonomous inspection technology is highly scalable.
There are over 12,000km pipelines and over 400 offshore platforms operating in Malaysian waters, all of which require periodic structural integrity inspections to ensure operational safety and production reliability.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chief statistician pays farewell visit to PM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today received a farewell courtesy visit from Chief Statistician of Malaysia Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin ahead of his mandatory retirement on July 8.
In a Facebook post, the prime minister expressed his appreciation for Uzir's service and dedication in strengthening the national statistical system, which serves as a key foundation for policymaking and Malaysia's development planning.
Anwar also shared photos of the visit, noting that Uzir was appointed Chief Statistician on Feb 14, 2017.
Uzir, who holds a PhD in Economics from the University of Sheffield, United Kingdom, joined the Department of Statistics in 1990.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Use legal routes, Thai Army tells Malaysians travelling to southern Thailand
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: The Thai army has assured Malaysians travelling to southern Thailand that their safety remains a priority, while urging people from both countries to use only legal border crossings.
Senior Expert of the Royal Thai Army and Chief of the Neighbouring Countries Coordination Centre, General Surathep Nukaeow, said the Thai military fully supports efforts by both Malaysia and Thailand to strengthen border security and curb illegal cross-border activities.
He said authorities on both sides share the same policies and objectives in ensuring the border remains safe and secure.
"We have a very good relationship with our Malaysian counterparts.
"We share the same ideas and policies, and we work closely together through various platforms, including the Regional Border Committee meetings," he told Malaysian reporters after visiting an illegal jetty here today.
Also present was National Security Council Land Border Management Division senior assistant director Colonel Kamal Kassim.
Surathep said there was no need for illegal cross-border activities as both countries have established legal entry points for the movement of people and goods.
"For the safety of everyone, we encourage both Malaysians and Thais to use only legal routes and comply with the laws and regulations of both countries," he said.
He said the public on both sides of the border should understand that the measures introduced by the authorities are intended to enhance security and safeguard the well-being of border communities.
Surathep also gave his assurance that Malaysian tourists visiting Thailand would continue to be safe, adding that Thai authorities remain committed to maintaining security and close cooperation with Malaysia along the shared border.
Kelantan is separated from Sungai Golok by the narrow Golok River, making cross-border travel convenient and attractive for many.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perkeso pays out RM1.2mil in first month of Lindung 24/7
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has disbursed more than RM1.2 million in benefits under its Non-Employment Injury Scheme (Lindung 24 Hours), within the first month of its implementation.
Perkeso said in a statement today that a total of 592 claims were received during the period.
The highest payout involves implant costs amounting to RM1.16 million, followed by temporary disablement benefit (TDB) payments totalling RM99,269.
"On average, nearly 20 cases a day were recorded under Lindung 24 Hours, reflecting Perkeso's efforts to close gaps in Malaysia's social protection system.
"Previously, accidents occurring outside working hours were not covered, but with Lindung 24 Hours, the situation has changed.
"The focus of social protection is no longer confined to commuting accidents or incidents at the workplace, but has become more comprehensive, extending coverage even when workers are at home," it said.
According to Perkeso, the expanded social security protection covers a range of benefits, including the TDB, which provides income replacement for insured persons who are medically certified unfit for work during their medical leave.
The scheme also covers medical and surgical treatment, implant costs, permanent disablement benefit based on assessments by the medical board, dependants' benefit, constant attendance allowance and rehabilitation services at Perkeso's rehabilitation centres.
Perkeso said off-duty protection for contributors under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4) is provided automatically, even though contribution deductions for the expanded coverage have yet to begin.
This underscores the role of Lindung 24 Hours as a more comprehensive social safety net for workers.
The organisation added that accidents outside working hours could result in significant medical expenses and prolonged loss of income, affecting the socio-economic well-being of families.
"As such, Perkeso remains committed to increasing public awareness of Lindung 24 Hours to ensure more workers understand its benefits while ensuring that no eligible worker is left without protection, including outside working hours," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Car wash owner among 4 arrested over assault on foreign worker
Article:
KUALA SELANGOR: Police have detained four people, including a car wash owner, after a foreign worker was assaulted at a car wash in Taman Kiara on Wednesday.
Kuala Selangor police chief Superintendent Azaharudin Tajudin said investigations showed a Toyota Hilux driver struck the worker with a traffic cone and punched him several times.
He said the suspect became angry because the victim was allegedly rude to him.
Azaharudin said following the incident, police detained the suspect, the car wash owner and two other foreign workers.
He said the two workers did not possess valid permits.
[[nid:1479537]]
The case is being investigated under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt using dangerous weapons, which carries a jail term of up to 10 years, a fine, caning, or any two of the punishments upon conviction.
The case is also being probed under Section 55B of the Immigration Act for employing a foreigner without a valid permit, and Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act for entering the country without a valid pass. Both offences carry jail terms and fines.
"Those with information on the incident are urged to contact the Kuala Selangor police headquarters at 03-3289 1222," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nestlé Malaysia accelerates with KitKat F1 campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd has launched its KitKat Formula 1 campaign in the local market, offering consumers exclusive promotions, limited-edition products and opportunities to win tickets to the 2026 Formula 1 Singapore Grand Prix.
The campaign is part of KitKat's global multi-year partnership with Formula 1, which coincides with the chocolate brand's 90th anniversary and Formula 1's 75th anniversary.
Nestlé Malaysia chief executive officer Juan Aranols said Formula 1 has become a global cultural phenomenon, attracting a growing number of fans across Malaysia and the wider region.
"Bringing this global partnership to life locally is a natural extension of KitKat's purpose – to remind people to pause, recharge, and savour the moment.
"We want every Malaysian, whether they have followed Formula 1 for years or are just discovering it, to feel the excitement of the sport and find their own break worth taking," he said at the event.
As part of the campaign, KitKat is holding consumer roadshows across several locations in the Klang Valley, featuring Formula 1-themed activities including racing simulators.
Consumers who buy at least RM10 worth of participating KitKat products and submit their receipts through the campaign microsite or WhatsApp will be eligible to enter a 12-week contest.
The grand prize is a full Formula 1 Singapore Airlines Singapore Grand Prix 2026 experience for two, while five first-prize winners will each receive a pair of three-day Pit Grandstand tickets.
Nestlé Malaysia has also introduced a limited-edition range of Formula 1-themed KitKat products, which are available at major retail outlets and online platforms nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TFP Solutions seals concert ticketing services with Pluotos Entertainment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: TFP Solutions Bhd has secured a concert ticketing services agreement with Pluotos Entertainment Sdn Bhd as it expands its presence in Malaysia's digital event technology and ticketing industry.
The company said its wholly-owned Softfac Technology Sdn Bhd signed a one-year ticketing services and strategic collaboration agreement with Pluotos on July 2.
Pluotos will provide the promotional and event information required for the ticketing platform, secure all necessary regulatory approvals and permits, and ensure venues have the infrastructure needed to support Softfac's ticketing operations.
TFP said the collaboration is in line with its strategy to expand its technology solutions business by strengthening its presence in the digital concert ticketing and event technology industry.
"The agreement secures the group a recurring, exclusive revenue stream from Pluotos' expanding pipeline of concert ticketed events," it said.
The deal also lays the groundwork for TFP to participate in a jointly owned concert ticketing platform and consumer database, supporting its expansion in the digital commerce and event technology space.
"Barring any unforeseen circumstances, the agreement is expected to contribute positively to TFP's consolidated earnings per share and net assets per share in the future," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Titijaya Land eyes expansion in Sabah with new projects in pipeline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Urban lifestyle developer Titijaya Land Bhd is strengthening its presence in Sabah as it seeks to capitalise on the state's growth prospects and diversify its property portfolio beyond the Klang Valley.
The company is preparing to launch several new projects in Sabah, including a commercial development in Likas, Kota Kinabalu.
It is also progressing with its purpose-built student accommodation project near Universiti Malaysia Sabah, aimed at addressing the shortage of quality student housing in the area.
At the same time, Titijaya expects continued growth from Citadines Waterfront Kota Kinabalu, which contributed a full year of operations in the financial year ended June 30, 2025, supported by steady occupancy rates and improving operational margins.
Managing director Lim Poh Yit said the company remains optimistic about Sabah's long-term prospects following the positive response to its existing projects in the state, including The Shore and Citadines Waterfront Kota Kinabalu.
He said recent strategic land acquisitions would strengthen the group's development pipeline and support future launches, including the upcoming Likas project.
According to Lim, Sabah continues to benefit from infrastructure improvements, rising tourist arrivals and growing business activity, creating demand for quality residential, commercial and hospitality developments.
Among Titijaya's flagship projects in the state is The Shore, a mixed-use development that includes residential, retail and hospitality components and houses Citadines Waterfront Kota Kinabalu. The hotel features 396 serviced units and caters to both business and leisure travellers.
The group is also undertaking a student accommodation development adjacent to UMS and the future Hospital UMS, which is expected to provide safer and more accessible housing options for students.
To date, Titijaya has completed projects with a combined gross development value of approximately RM5.4 billion, spanning high-rise residential, integrated, commercial, SoHo and industrial developments.
The group said it remains focused on long-term value creation through disciplined execution, prudent capital management and innovation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court bars Chegubard from publishing statements on army general
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has granted an interim injunction restraining activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, from publishing or disseminating statements concerning Major-General Faridhulathroth Hashim pending the disposal of a defamation suit filed against him.
Faridhulathroth, 52, the assistant chief of staff (intelligence), filed the suit against Badrul Hisham, 48, on April 21.
The army general's lawyer Mohamed Baharudeen Mohamed Ariff said judge Roslan Mat Nor allowed the interim injunction application during proceedings on Tuesday.
The suit arises from six allegedly defamatory statements published on Badrul Hisham's Facebook account between Dec 31, 2025 and Jan 17 concerning Faridhulathroth's alleged criminal activities and misconduct.
Baharudeen said the interim injunction will remain in force until the disposal of the suit.
"The order restrains Badrul Hisham and his representatives from publishing, republishing, disseminating or further disseminating any statements, images or references concerning the plaintiff, save where necessary for the conduct of court proceedings.
"The court further directed that the relevant online publications and references under the defendant's control be hidden," he said.
The court also awarded Faridhulathroth costs of RM3,000.
Also appearing for the plaintiff were Nur-Diana Ezlyn Hashim and Sureshmurti Ramani.
Badrul Hisham was represented by Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, Amirul Ar-Rasyid and Akif Rusli.
Rafique confirmed the court's decision.
In his statement of claim, Faridhulathroth alleged that the six statements were false, baseless and published maliciously with the intention of damaging his personal and professional reputation.
He claimed the publications portrayed him as a senior military officer involved in criminal activities and serious misconduct, lacking integrity, abusing public office and being a mastermind behind corruption at the highest levels of the army.
According to the statement of claim, the publications exposed him to hatred, contempt, ridicule and embarrassment, causing substantial prejudice to his reputation and standing.
Faridhulathroth further alleged that Badrul Hisham failed to take reasonable steps to verify the allegations before publishing them and deliberately disseminated the statements to sensationalise the claims and attract followers, despite receiving a letter of demand.
He said the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission informed him that its investigation had found no criminal evidence against him and that the prosecution had decided not to bring any charges.
He is seeking general, aggravated and exemplary damages, together with such further or other relief as the court deems fit.
The next case management has been fixed for Oct 26.
Earlier this year, Faridhulathroth lodged
a police report
against Badrul Hisham.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Transport Ministry reviewing taxi sector under Klang Valley transport plan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Transport Ministry is implementing three proactive initiatives to rejuvenate the taxi industry, including a review under the Klang Valley public transport strategic plan.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the study would determine the future direction of the industry, including a review of taxi fares.
He said the other measures involve developing a taxi vehicle financing package with the national car manufacturer and providing training for drivers.
"The ministry is collaborating with the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry and the Malaysian Road Safety Council to enhance service quality and professionalism," he said at the launch of the National Madani Taxi programme officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Loke said the programme marks an important step toward transforming the taxi industry into a modern, competitive sector capable of playing a key role in the public transport ecosystem.
He said the Proton S70 has been selected as the new official taxi model based on its safety standards, passenger comfort and fuel efficiency.
Under the programme, Loke said the financing model allows drivers to own their vehicles outright, replacing the long-standing leasing system.
"The special approval granted by the Finance Ministry allows taxi drivers to become the legal owners of their new vehicles, effectively ending the traditional leasing model," he said.
"This is not the old leasing scheme. The owner or driver will be the legal owner of the vehicle, even if it is financed through a financial institution. Ownership will remain with the driver," he said.
Loke added that Malaysian taxis will adopt a modern identity without the traditional roof-mounted sign, and will instead use a special registration number series beginning with the letters "GET".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATE] Anwar: Any US pardon for Jho Low won't affect Malaysia's stance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said Malaysia will not change its position on Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, even if the fugitive businessman is granted a presidential pardon in the United States.
"That is a decision for the US," Anwar said when asked about the possibility of a presidential pardon for Low, the alleged mastermind behind the multibillion-dollar 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal.
"But our position is not affected by any decision in the US," he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid As-Sodiqin in Cheras.
Anwar had previously said the government was not considering the possibility of granting a pardon to Low.
The 1MDB state investment fund was set up by former Malaysian prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak in 2009, shortly after he took office.
Investigators allege that more than US$4.5 billion was misappropriated from the fund between 2009 and 2015 by officials and associates, including fugitive financier Jho Low.
Najib has been convicted in several cases linked to the scandal and is serving a prison sentence after being fined for offences related to the misuse of 1MDB funds.
His lawyers have denied that he was the mastermind behind the scheme, instead placing the blame on Low.
Media organisation The Atlantic
reported yesterday
that Low could be among those being considered for clemency by US President Donald Trump as the country prepares to celebrate its 250th Independence Day tomorrow.
Low faces multiple charges, including corruption and money laundering, in the US and Malaysia over his alleged role in the misappropriation of 1MDB funds.
He has consistently denied wrongdoing, while his whereabouts remain unknown.
The Wall Street Journal
reported
that Low filed a pardon request in May seeking the dismissal of his US criminal charges.
The US Department of Justice website lists a pending request under Low for a "Pardon after Completion of Sentence" filed this year.
However, a White House official reportedly said Low's request was not currently on its radar.
Meanwhile, regarding the US government's RM1 million grant to help restore Tugu Negara, Anwar said the matter would be discussed by the Cabinet next week.
Yesterday, US Embassy Chargé d'Affaires ad interim David H. Gamble said the US would
contribute RM1 million
towards the restoration of Tugu Negara, symbolising bilateral ties as it celebrates its 250th Independence Day and Malaysia approaches its 70th Merdeka anniversary.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Only King can revoke Azam Baki's NFCC board appointment, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Only Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia has the authority to revoke Tan Sri Azam Baki's appointment as a member of the National Financial Crime Centre (NFCC) advisory board, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Anwar said the appointment was made by the King and remains valid until 2027.
"The appointment ends in 2027, and only the King can revoke it. It has nothing to do with the MACC chief commissioner," he told reporters after Friday prayers today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: At least two dead in Russian border regions after Ukrainian drone attacks
Article:
MOSCOW: At least two people were killed in Russia's border regions and several industrial sites suffered damage during Ukrainian drone attacks overnight, local authorities and media said on Friday.
Ukraine has pounded Russia's energy infrastructure for months in efforts to cripple Moscow's military might.
The attacks have led to fuel shortages across Russia, the world's largest country.
Russian President Vladimir Putin has said the attacks are meant to sow discord among the public.
Separately, Ukrainian officials said on Telegram that four people were killed and a further 10 people were injured in Russian overnight attacks on Ukraine.
Valentin Demidov, the mayor of Russia's western city of Belgorod, said a woman had died in a car after being injured by shrapnel.
He said water and power supplies in the city, which is about 40 km (25 miles) north of the border with Ukraine, had been disrupted.
Earlier today, Vesti news channel, citing local officials, said an industrial facility had caught fire after Ukraine attacked Belgorod and its surroundings with missiles.
Egor Kovalchuk, the acting governor of Bryansk, another Russian border region, said a man had died in a village following a kamikaze drone attack.
Fire also broke out at an industrial site in the western Smolensk region following a drone attack there, though no one was hurt, the local governor, Vasily Anokhin, wrote on Telegram. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stop 'old' attacks on Umno, BN, Zahid tells unity govt allies
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has urged his unity government allies not to raise old issues targeting Umno and the coalition during the Johor election campaign.
"Alhamdulillah, our campaign is gaining momentum. Although we are facing our friends in Johor, the issues have been managed well.
"Some are attacking Umno and BN using old issues, but I hope these will not be played up as we are partners in the unity government," he said after meeting residents in Kempas.
Zahid said he did not want any party to face awkwardness when they returned to the cabinet after the election.
He said each party should focus on highlighting the strengths of its own candidates instead of reviving old issues, given that cabinet ministers meet every week.
Zahid also said BN was not overconfident about securing a landslide victory. Instead, the coalition viewed itself as the underdog and would continue working hard to increase its seat tally.
He said Johor's changing political landscape, with more than 50 per cent of voters comprising youths, required BN to focus on their needs through its manifesto.
"Among our main priorities is creating more premium-salary jobs by strengthening technical and vocational education and training, while also expanding skills training opportunities for the younger generation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zero tolerance for wrongdoing, vows Prison Dept after 5 charged with assault
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Prisons Department will not compromise on any wrongdoing by its staff following the charging of five warders at the Taiping magistrate's court in Perak.
The department said it respected the legal process and would continue to cooperate with the police, the Attorney-General's Chambers, and the judiciary to ensure justice is served.
"The Prisons Department upholds the principles of integrity, professionalism, and accountability in executing its responsibilities as the leader of the country's corrective services.
"We will not compromise on any misconduct that violates the law or compromises public trust in the institution," it said in a statement.
The department added that comments that could affect proceedings should be avoided.
"This is in line with the principle of the rule of law and the presumption of innocence until proven guilty by the court," it said.
Earlier today, the five warders
pleaded not guilty
to assaulting three inmates last year.
Mohd Hairie Jumri, 40, and Saiful Azman Mohamad Ibrahim, 44, were charged under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt using a dangerous weapon. The offence provides for a jail term of up to 10 years, a fine, whipping, or any two of the punishments upon conviction.
Ahmad Rizal Razali, 37, Muhamad Mustakhim Abdul Rahim, 40, and Nor Hafiz Izwan Muhammad Jajam, 31, pleaded not guilty to charges under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt. The section carries a maximum one year's imprisonment, a fine of up to RM2,000, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Najib, son to know on Sept 4 outcome of stay bid in tax case
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Seri Najib Razak and his son Datuk Nazifuddin Najib will know on Sept 4 whether the Court of Appeal will allow their bid to stay bankruptcy proceedings initiated by the Inland Revenue Board (IRB) over RM1.7 billion in unpaid taxes.
A three-member bench chaired by Datuk Dr Alwi Abdul Wahab reserved its decision today after hearing submissions from counsel for both parties.
The other members of the bench were Datuk Dr Shahnaz Sulaiman and Datuk Ong Chee Kwan.
The appeal is against the High Court's decision on Nov 17 last year dismissing Najib and Nazifuddin's application to stay the bankruptcy proceedings pending the disposal of their tax appeals before the Special Commissioners of Income Tax (SCIT).
Senior lawyer Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, who represented the duo, argued that Najib's additional tax assessment of RM1.69 billion was imposed despite him having paid income tax.
He said the assessment was largely based on funds received from Saudi Arabia, which were later returned after the general election.
Shafee argued that the funds should have been treated as tax-exempt donations but were wrongly classified as taxable income.
"We have raised this issue from the very beginning, but the IRB has never explained how it arrived at the additional tax assessment," he said.
He urged the court to stay the bankruptcy proceedings pending the SCIT hearing, where Najib intends to challenge the validity of the tax assessment.
Shafee added that a bankruptcy order would prejudice his client by making it more difficult to engage legal representation and finance his legal expenses.
Senior revenue counsel Norhisham Ahmad, who appeared for the IRB, argued that the amount of tax claimed, although substantial, did not constitute a special circumstance warranting a stay.
He said the law was clear that taxpayers must pay the assessed tax first before disputing it.
"In law, he must pay first and dispute it later," he said.
In 2020, the High Court entered summary judgments ordering Najib and Nazifuddin to pay RM1.69 billion and RM37.6 million respectively in unpaid taxes for the assessment years between 2011 and 2017.
The Federal Court upheld the judgments on Oct 16, 2023, ruling that taxes assessed under the Income Tax Act remain payable notwithstanding any pending appeal.
The IRB subsequently commenced bankruptcy proceedings after the pair failed to settle the outstanding tax liabilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mesiniaga bags RM40mil job to provide centralised server infra
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mesiniaga Bhd has secured a RM39.99 million contract from Pharmaniaga Logistics Sdn Bhd to deliver a new centralised server infrastructure for the Pharmacy Information System (PhIS) Version 3.0.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the technology and digital solutions provider said the contract is scheduled for completion by June 30, 2030.
The contract does not include an automatic renewal clause.
Mesiniaga said the risks associated with the contract relate to meeting the customer's service level commitments and project deadlines, adding that it has taken the necessary measures to mitigate these risks.
The contract is expected to contribute to the group's earnings and net assets from the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026 until the completion of the project in 2030.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singapore driver fined RM20,000 for filling RON95 petrol
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A foreign motorist was fined RM20,000, or three months' jail in default, by the Sessions Court here for illegally filling his Singapore-registered vehicle with subsidised RON95 petrol.
Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim passed the sentence yesterday after the accused, in his 50s and the driver and owner of the vehicle, pleaded guilty to the charge.
The accused paid the fine on the same day.
The offence occurred on April 9, when Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers, conducting Op Tiris 4.0, caught the Singaporean filling his vehicle with BUDI95 at a petrol station here.
Investigations revealed that the driver committed an offence under the Control of Supplies Act 1961 by purchasing subsidised RON95 petrol using a foreign-registered vehicle.
Deputy public prosecutor Nur Sulehan Abd Rahman prosecuted, while the accused was represented by defence counsel.
The ministry's Johor chapter director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said the successful prosecution proved the ministry's continuous commitment to combating the misappropriation of controlled goods to protect consumer interests and ensure national supply stability.
The public is encouraged to report any misappropriation of fuel through official the ministry's channels, including WhatsApp at 019-848 8000, the e-Aduan portal at e-aduan@kpdn.gov.my, the call centre at 1-800-886-800, or the Ez ADU KPDN smartphone application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang to study order to disclose RM1bil Jelutong landfill agreement
Article:
BALIK PULAU: The Penang government will study an order to disclose the agreement for the RM1 billion Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and reclamation project before deciding its next course of action.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said any party disagreeing with the Freedom of Information (FOI) Appeals Board's ruling to disclose the agreement still had legal options.
"The state government will study the issue first before making a decision," he said when asked if the state would make the agreement public.
"This is only one stage because our judicial system has several levels. Any party that feels a different decision is warranted can bring the matter before the higher judicial bodies," he said.
The state FOI Appeals Board on Tuesday ordered the state secretary's office to release the agreement after ruling that it did not fall under exempt documents.
The board found that authorities had failed to show how disclosing the agreement would harm the economic interests of the state or its agencies.
The ruling followed an appeal by former Protect Karpal Drive chairman A.D. Chandrasekaran, whose request for the document was previously rejected by the state secretary's office.
The project to rehabilitate the former Jelutong landfill and adjacent land reclamation was awarded to PLB Engineering Bhd under a joint development agreement signed in 2020 involving the Penang Development Corporation, the state government and the company.
The project has drawn widespread objections, with Chow and his predecessor Lim Guan Eng clashing over the matter.
Lim
questioned
why Chow had yet to fulfil his promise to cancel the joint development agreement despite repeated delays and the rejection of the project's Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA).
He said the state government had granted four extensions for the project since the agreement was signed with PLB Engineering on Feb 21, 2020, with the latest extension ending on Feb 26 this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Paris Baguette disposal clears earnings drag for Berjaya Food: CIMB Securities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Food Bhd's disposal of its loss-making joint venture Berjaya Paris Baguette (BPB) is positive for its turnaround story, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd.
Its analyst Walter Aw Lik Hsin said the exit removes a "chronic drag" on company earnings, although the near-term profit and loss impact is expected to be limited since the joint venture's (JV) carrying value was already close to zero.
"We are positive on the disposal, as it removes a loss-making non-core JV from Berjaya Food's portfolio and should allow management to focus on Starbucks Malaysia, its key earnings driver with 301 stores as of June 30, 2025.
"The disposal reinforces our view that Berjaya Food is pruning underperforming units as part of its turnaround, consistent with the narrowing of losses seen in the third quarter of financial year 2026," he said.
Berjaya Food on Tuesday disposed its 50 per cent stake, or 20 million shares, in Berjaya Paris Baguette to Paris Baguette Singapore, the franchisor and remaining 50 per cent shareholder, for a nominal RM1.
The company also settled RM3.9 million of BPB's liabilities, including hire-purchase obligations owed to Prime Credit Leasing, a related party under Berjaya Corp Bhd.
No additional liabilities were assumed and the deal did not require shareholder or regulatory approval.
BPB has been loss-making since its launch in January 2023, with losses widening to RM7.0 million in the first nine months of financial year 2026 (FY26), compared with RM3.4 million in the corresponding period of FY25.
CIMB Securities said BPB was Berjaya Food's only joint venture, with the original investment cost of RM20 million for the 50 per cent stake, incurred since July 2022, has already been fully impaired.
Based on FY25 accounts, the net asset value of Berjaya Food's stake stood at RM8.1 million as at June 30 2025, slightly lower than RM8.5 million previously, reflecting continued losses at the JV level.
CIMB Securities noted that Berjaya Food still retains exposure to other Paris Baguette-related ventures.
This includes a 60 per cent stake in the Philippines JV established in August 2023 and a 70 per cent stake in a Brunei JV formed in October 2024.
Despite the disposal, Berjaya Food guided that the transaction will not have a material impact on FY26 earnings, net assets, or gearing.
CIMB Securities maintained its forecasts and valuation, keeping FY26-FY28 core net loss projections unchanged.
The firm maintained a "Hold" call on Berjaya Food with an unchanged target price of 21 sen, saying the worst may be behind the stock while valuation remains fair.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia extends midday gains
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its gains at midday, tracking advances across regional markets as sustained buying interest in heavyweight counters continued to provide support.
At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) leapt 14.31 points, or 0.86 per cent, to 1,676.14, from Thursday's close of 1,661.83.
The benchmark index opened 3.90 points higher at 1,665.73 and traded between 1,662.91 and 1,677.65 during the session.
Market breadth was positive, with gainers outnumbering losers by 499 to 368, while 475 counters were unchanged, 1,308 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover stood at 1.67 billion shares worth RM899.83 million.
Regionally, Shanghai's SSE Composite Index rose 0.69 per cent to 4,056.78, Japan's Nikkei 225 gained 1.28 per cent to 69,614.16, South Korea's KOSPI Composite Index advanced 4.56 per cent to 7,996.82, and Singapore's Straits Times Index rose 0.36 per cent to 5,235.90.
Apex Securities Bhd said in a note today that the softer United States (US) payrolls print rewrites the near-term policy calculus, draining urgency from the US interest rate-hike trade and giving emerging-market currencies room to breathe, with the ringgit up 0.33 per cent against the dollar even as foreign equity money continued to flow out.
It said banks deserve close attention for follow-through as receding US hike risk and a firmer ringgit provide the sector with a more favourable operating backdrop, and the sector's leadership is what any index recovery currently rests on.
Among heavyweight stocks, Maybank gained 12 sen to RM10.86, CIMB added 10 sen to RM7.55,  Tenaga Nasional increased four sen to RM14.30, and Public Bank was one sen higher at RM4.85, while IHH Healthcare was flat at RM8.17.
As for the active stocks, Velesto bagged 2.5 sen to 29 sen, Eckem Holdings inched up half a sen to 12.5 sen, Zetrix AI increased 1.5 sen to 79.5 sen, Silver Ridge gained four sen to 21 sen, while Tanco was flat at 17.5 sen.
On the gainers list, Binastra climbed eight sen to RM2.14, F&N jumped 68 sen to RM28.78, KL Kepong added 52 sen to RM21.48, Dutch Lady soared 24 sen to RM32.30, and PPB bagged 16 sen to RM9.49.
Of the decliners, Nestle shed 92 sen to RM91.08, Malaysian Pacific Industries lost 32 sen to RM47.74, United Plantations declined 22 sen to RM33.26, Far East dropped nine sen to RM3.81, and Gas Malaysia trimmed seven sen to RM5.13.
On the broader market, the FBM Emas Index advanced 88.01 points to 12,438.68, the FBM Top 100 Index rose 87.02 points to 12,266.65, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index firmed 79.25 points to 12,284.30.
The FBM Mid 70 Index climbed 51.26 points to 17,835.95, while the FBM ACE Index advanced 12.65 points to 4,866.10.
Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index surged 150.10 points to 19,776.34, the Industrial Products and Services Index went up 2.60 points to 182.05, and the Energy Index gained 7.70 points to 757.36, while the Plantation Index grew 51.10 points to 8,939.51.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chinese robot maker Unitree wins approval for US$619 million Shanghai IPO
Article:
SINGAPORE: Chinese robot maker Unitree Robotics has received regulatory approval for a Shanghai listing through which it plans to raise 4.2 billion yuan (US$619.4 million), moving it closer to one of China's most watched technology initial public offerings.
The approval comes as China's onshore IPO market shows signs of life after a long slowdown. China Resources New Energy shares more than doubled in their Shenzhen debut on Thursday after the wind and solar company raised 24.5 billion yuan, Asia's biggest IPO so far this year.
China's securities regulator said in a Thursday notice that it had approved Unitree's application for the IPO on Shanghai's STAR Market, the city's technology board. The approval is valid for 12 months.
A-share IPOs, or listings on the Shanghai, Shenzhen and Beijing exchanges, raised US$7.7 billion in the first half of the year, up 64.4 per cent from the year-earlier period, LSEG data showed.
Unitree plans to sell at least 40.45 million shares, according to its prospectus filed with the Shanghai Stock Exchange previously. The company has not set a launch date or price range.
Unitree plans to use the proceeds for robot artificial intelligence model research, robot body research, new robot product development and a smart robot manufacturing base, according to the prospectus.
The Hangzhou-based company makes humanoid robots, four-legged robots and robot parts.
Its listing would test investor demand for China's robotics sector, a key part of Beijing's push to develop machines that can use AI in factories, homes and public spaces.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor to expand Bangsa Johor school initiative to religious schools
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor is expanding its Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor (SRBJ) initiative to religious schools as part of efforts to transform Islamic education in the state.
Caretaker Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the Bangsa Johor Religious Pioneer School initiative would complement the four SRBJ schools already in operation.
"We have already established four pioneer schools, comprising two primary and two secondary schools, and we will now establish one for religious education.
"We will not stop there. We also plan to introduce the initiative at kindergartens in collaboration with Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi," he said after officiating the Johor Religious Teachers' Day celebration.
Meanwhile, caretaker Johor Islamic religious affairs committee chairman Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid said the first Bangsa Johor religious school would be located in Kota Iskandar, Iskandar Puteri, and would begin operations this year.
He said the school would be equipped with digital learning facilities and staffed by selected teachers, with similar schools to be established in every district over time.
SRBJ is an educational transformation initiative inspired by Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, Regent of Johor.
The four existing pioneer schools are Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Seri Kota Puteri 4 and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Seri Kota Puteri 2 in Pasir Gudang, as well as SK Tasek Utara and SMK Tasek Utara in Johor Baru.
Onn Hafiz also launched the Johor Religious Teachers' Code of Ethics and presented the State Islamic Education Leadership Award and the Johor Government Religious Teacher Leadership Award.
He also endorsed six resolutions adopted at the Johor Islamic Education Symposium 2026, which he said would be submitted for royal assent.
"After carefully listening to the resolutions presented, we will bring these six resolutions for the assent of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, Sultan Ibrahim, and Tunku Mahkota Ismail," he said in his speech.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China stocks rebound after selloff; Hong Kong extends gains on Fed rate outlook
Article:
SHANGHAI: China stocks rebounded on Friday after a sharp selloff in chipmakers in the previous session, while Hong Kong shares extended gains as a tepid US jobs report reinforced bets that the Federal Reserve would keep monetary policy supportive.
As of midday, the Shanghai Composite index inched up 0.6 per cent, while the blue-chip CSI300 index gained 1.2 per cent.
The tech-focused STAR 50 index, which suffered its biggest one-day decline since April 2025 on Thursday, advanced 1.1 per cent.
Robotics shares led gains, with a sector sub-index jumping 5.7 per cent after China's securities regulator approved Unitree's Shanghai IPO late on Thursday.
In Hong Kong, the Hang Seng Index advanced 1.6 per cent, while the city's tech shares jumped 2 per cent.
US job growth slowed sharply in June, signalling a cooling labour market and prompting investors to scale back expectations for higher US interest rates.
"We believe the incrementally bigger representation of the tech- and innovation-heavy sectors in the A-share market will continue, with clear policy, funding, resources and capital market action support," analysts at Morgan Stanley said in a note.
"As a result, we see the best opportunities at the index level as offered by the A-share market."
Investors are closely watching June economic data due in the coming weeks for fresh clues on the strength of China's economic recovery.
"Monthly indicators could show signs of stabilisation," Citi analysts said in a note.
"Investment remains a drag... This set of data is unlikely to shift the tone of the mid-year Politburo meeting."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 5,140 of Malaysia's new passports issued since Wednesday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has issued 5,140 copies of the new Malaysian passport since Wednesday.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said applications at 14 passport issuing offices nationwide remained manageable.
Zakaria said that the public understood that the old passports remained valid for use up to six months before their expiry dates, meaning there was no need to rush to replace them.
The
new passport features
94 security characteristics, up from 49 previously.
Security technologies used include guilloche, latent image, rainbow printing, line width modulation, holograms, and ultraviolet printing, all designed to enhance protection against forgery, tampering, and misuse.
Zakaria previously said the new security features involved visual, hidden, and forensic elements, in line with technological advancements and increasingly complex global security requirements.
He said the new passport with a 10-year validity period costs RM350, while the fee for the five-year validity period remains at RM200.
For a start, the passports will be issued at 14 state offices, including the headquarters, for the first phase.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New taxi financing model ends leasing system, says Loke
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Taxi drivers participating in the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme will now own their vehicles outright under a new financing model, replacing the long-standing leasing system.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said a special approval from the Finance Ministry allows drivers to become the legal owners of their new taxis, marking the end of the traditional leasing practice in the industry.
"This is not the old scheme or the old leasing model. The owner or driver is the legal owner of the vehicle, even if it is financed through a financial institution. Ownership remains with the driver," he said at the
programme launch today
.
Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud.
Under the programme, the Proton S70 sedan has been selected as the new official taxi model, based on its safety features, passenger comfort and fuel efficiency.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proton S70 named official taxi under Madani reform
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Proton S70 has been selected as the official taxi model under the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme, marking a key shift in the modernisation of the country's taxi industry.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the sedan was chosen based on its safety features, passenger comfort and fuel efficiency, making it suitable for daily public transport use.
He said this was part of broader efforts to enhance the image and competitiveness of the taxi industry.
"The new taxis will feature a more modern look, without the traditional roof-mounted taxi topper, and will carry a special registration number series beginning with the letters 'GET'," he said.
He said the reform programme also introduces a new financing model that allows drivers to own their vehicles outright, replacing the long-standing leasing system.
To help increase drivers' income, he said the ministry, in collaboration with the private sector, will introduce digital advertising screens inside taxi cabins as an additional revenue stream.
He said the initiative will also be supported by the integration of digital booking systems through e-hailing platforms.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim
launched the programme
at Dataran Merdeka today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH wants Johor to become economic powerhouse, not just 'feeder' territory
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan wants Johor to emerge as an economic powerhouse instead of merely as a "feeder" territory to Singapore.
PH election machinery director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said Johor's future development must be planned with the goal of creating more quality jobs so that people in the state no longer work in Singapore or abroad to earn a living.
Amirudin said Johor must become a state capable of generating its own opportunities, talent, and wealth.
"Hundreds of thousands of locals should not have to cross the Causeway every day to earn a living.
"We will ensure that Johor offers enough for Johoreans and fellow Malaysians to secure high-paying jobs here," he said in his speech at the launch of the PH manifesto for the Johor election here today.
Also present were Johor PH chairman and state Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan, Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching, and Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa.
Amirudin said Johor has the capacity to stand strong independently and has the potential to become one of the most critical growth hubs in Southeast Asia if state and federal strengths are effectively combined.
"When people look at Malaysia from Singapore, and they cross the Causeway, they should say Malaysia is great because they see how exceptional Johor is.
"Johor should stand as a symbol of Malaysia's success and dignity," he said.
In line with this, Amirudin said that PH's development philosophy for Johor must ensure that economic growth is distributed equitably across the entire state.
"A Johor that is fair to all, with economic development that benefits everyone. We want to develop every locality and district," he said.
Meanwhile, Amirudin acknowledged that several ongoing strategic projects—including the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, and the revival of Forest City—had already generated positive growth momentum capable of transforming the state's economic landscape.
However, Amirudin said that the benefits of this development must translate into high-value job opportunities and increased income for the people across the whole of Johor, rather than being concentrated solely in the southern region.S
"Our future menteri besar will be a menteri besar for all state constituencies.
"A menteri besar for every district we intend to develop. We will empower northern Johor, elevate central Johor, and strengthen southern Johor," he said.
Amirudin said while Johor Baru records a higher average income, several other areas — including the Mersing district and the town of Benut — still register significantly lower income levels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM: RM10mil approved for old taxi replacement scheme
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has approved an additional RM10 million for the matching grant programme to replace old taxis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today.
Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said the additional allocation would support the National MADANI Taxi Reform Programme, which is expected to raise drivers' monthly net income through lower repair, maintenance, servicing and financing costs.
"There was one point raised, which is taxi income. It was noted that at times, earnings can fall to between RM2,500 and RM3,000 depending on the season. Typically, drivers earn about RM4,000, and reaching RM5,000 a month is very difficult.
"But under this scheme, I discussed with Anthony Loke that there must be a proper plan.
"Under this new scheme, by working together to reduce repair, maintenance, servicing and financing costs, we expect drivers to earn between RM5,000 and RM7,000 a month, allowing drivers to live a more comfortable life.
"Since the response has been good, I approved an additional RM10 million today, specifically for taxis," he said when launching the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme here today.
A similar initiative was implemented under this year's Budget, with the same amount set aside to encourage the public to replace vehicles aged 20 years and above, with incentives of up to RM4,000.
Present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
Earlier, Loke in his speech had requested additional allocation after the RM10 million set aside under the 2026 Budget was fully utilised within three months.
The reform package also introduces a new financing model that allows taxi drivers to own their vehicles outright, replacing the long-standing leasing system.
Loke said special approval from the Finance Ministry allows drivers to become the legal owners of their new taxis, marking
the end of the traditional leasing practice
in the industry.
Under the same programme, the Proton S70 has been selected as the new
official taxi model
based on its safety features, passenger comfort and fuel efficiency.
Meanwhile, Anwar said the government was also reviewing the taxi permit system to allow permits to be issued directly to drivers instead of companies.
"If you look back at my speeches in Parliament over the years, one of the issues I have consistently raised is why taxi permits were given to wealthy individuals while drivers were merely expected to earn a halal livelihood.
"Why can't the permits be issued directly to the drivers so that they become the owners?
"This is an issue that has been raised for decades. The government now believes that permits should be granted to those who are capable of operating the service," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India's HCLTech wins US$1.14 billion deal with European firm
Article:
BENGALURU: India's HCLTech has won a US$1.14 billion deal with a major European firm, the software services exporter said on Friday.
HCLTech's shares opened 4.6 per cent higher on Friday after the announcement, boosting gains on the Nifty IT index, which was up 2.5 per cent.
Here are some details:
The deal will help HCLTech establish an AI-driven operating model to transform and manage the client's global digital workplace and enterprise networks, the company said.
The deal will last for four-and-a-half years, and is extendable by another five years, it added, without providing further details about the European firm.
This is entirely new business for the company, HCLTech said, and not from an existing client or a deal renewal.
HCLTech, which will report its results for the first quarter of fiscal 2027 on July 13, has forecast revenue growth of 1 per cent-4 per cent for the year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court of Appeal upholds ruling that DAP’s Chong Chieng Jen defamed Sarawak government
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Court of Appeal has unanimously dismissed an appeal by Sarawak DAP chief Chong Chieng Jen, upholding a High Court ruling that he defamed the Sarawak government and the Sarawak Financial Authority (SFA) over claims regarding state funds in 2013.
The three-judge panel increased the damages awarded to the state government and the SFA from RM150,000 to RM300,000. The court also ordered the Stampin MP to pay RM150,000 in costs to the respondents,
Free Malaysia Today
reported.
Chong said following the decision that he is considering filing an appeal to the Federal Court.
The suit was filed in 2013 after Chong alleged that RM11 billion in state funds had disappeared into a “black hole.”
The case went to trial in January 2021 and concluded in July of the same year. In August 2022, the High Court ruled that the claims were defamatory.
During the appeal, Chong’s lawyer, Michael Kong, argued for a retrial, contending that the trial judge erred in setting aside a subpoena for former state finance minister II Wong Soon Koh, which prevented him from testifying for the defence.
Counsel for the Sarawak government and the SFA argued that the trial was conducted properly and that a retrial would prejudice the plaintiffs and inconvenience witnesses.

Title: Five Taiping Prison officers charged over alleged baton attacks on inmates
Article:
TAIPING, July 3 — Five prison officers were charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with injuring three inmates at Taiping Prison in separate incidents last January.
All of the accused — Mohd Hairie Jumri, 40; Saiful Azman Mohamad Ibrahim, 44; Ahmad Rizal Razali, 37; Muhamad Mustakhim Abdul Rahim, 40; and Nor Hafiz Izwan Muhammad Jajam, 31 — pleaded not guilty to the charges.
In the court before Magistrate R. Prabakaran, Mohd Hairie and Saiful Azman are jointly charged with intentionally causing hurt to P. Jason Immanuel, 25, using a baton at Hall B, Taiping Prison, Jalan Taming Sari here, between 3pm and 4pm on Jan 17, 2025.
The charge was brought under Section 324 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Act, which provides for a maximum prison sentence of 10 years, a fine, or whipping, or any two of the punishments, upon conviction.
The two prison officers, an inspector and a sergeant, respectively, were allowed bail of RM4,000 each with two sureties and ordered to report to a nearby police station on the first day of every month pending the disposal of the case.
The court set Sept 7 for mention and the submission of documents.
In the same court, Ahmad Rizal, also a prison inspector, was charged with voluntarily causing hurt to V. Naveen Kumar, 32, at the same location between 3pm and 4.15pm on the same date.
The charge was brought under Section 323 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum penalty of one year in prison, a fine of up to RM2,000, or both, upon conviction.
He was allowed bail of RM3,000 with two sureties. The court set Sept 7 for the submission of documents.
The accused were represented by lawyer Aqmarul Aqil Ismail, while Deputy Public Prosecutor Farihah Adilah Fazial prosecuted.
In the court before Magistrate Ahmad Hamdi Mustafar, Muhamad Mustakhim and Nor Hafiz Izwan were jointly charged under Section 323 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same law, with voluntarily causing hurt to G. Yugarajan, 30, between 3pm and 4pm at the same location and date.
Magistrate Ahmad Hamdi allowed them bail of RM2,000 with one surety each and fixed Sept 8 for mention for the submission of documents.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Nazura Azman prosecuted, while lawyers Amirul Fairuzzeen Jamaluddin and Mohd Aidil Arsad represented Muhamad Mustakhim, and Nor Hafiz Izwan was represented by lawyer Aqmarul Aqil Ismail. — Bernama

Title: Shafee tells court Najib’s tax appeal would be moot if LHDN insists on ‘pay first, talk later’
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Lawyer Tan Sri Shafee Abdullah argued before the Court of Appeal today that failure to stay the Inland Revenue Board’s (LHDN) case against ex-prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak would render his appeal before the Special Commissioners of Income Tax (SCIT) in September “meaningless.”
Speaking before a three-member bench chaired by Justice Datuk Alwi Abdul Wahab, Shafee contended that proceeding with the bankruptcy action would be “akin to entering the boxing ring with both hands tied,”
Free Malaysia Today
reported.
He warned that a bankruptcy declaration would not only bar Najib from various activities but would also strip him of control over his assets, placing them under the authority of the director-general of insolvency.
The dispute centres on an additional tax assessment of RM1.69 billion for the 2011 to 2017 period, stemming from an alleged RM8 billion donation that Najib maintains was used for political and welfare activities.
The LHDN, represented by senior revenue counsel Norhisham Ahmad, countered that there are “no special circumstances” to justify a stay.
Ahmad leaned on a Federal Court ruling from October 2023 that upheld the “pay first, talk later” principle, which mandates that tax assessments must be settled regardless of any pending appeals.
The bench, which includes Justices Datuk Shahnaz Sulaiman and Datuk Ong Chee Kwan, has set September 4 for its decision.
This hearing follows a November 17 ruling last year, in which the High Court dismissed applications by Najib and his son, Nazifuddin, to stay bankruptcy proceedings over unpaid tax arrears of RM1.69 billion and RM37.6 million, respectively.
The court affirmed that pending appeals do not shield taxpayers from recovery actions.
Najib continues to challenge two bankruptcy notices, arguing that the assessments, which climbed from RM1.46 billion to RM1.69 billion due to penalties and interest, are flawed and should not be enforced while related proceedings remain active.

Title: Home minister: Police working with Interpol to locate Na’imah, children in Europe in ongoing probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) are tracking the whereabouts of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children, who are believed to be in Europe, to assist in an ongoing police investigation.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said intelligence and preliminary checks by the authorities indicated that they are currently overseas.
“At this stage, PDRM is actively working to locate the late Tun Daim’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children to assist in completing the ongoing police investigation.
“Based on intelligence and preliminary checks by the authorities, the individuals in question are believed to be overseas, specifically in Europe,” he said in a written parliamentary reply posted on the Dewan Rakyat website on Thursday.
He was responding to a question from Lim Lip Eng (PH–Kepong), who wanted to know whether members of the late Tun Daim’s family were being sought by police due to their presence abroad, and what measures the government was taking to bring them back to Malaysia.
Saifuddin Nasution said PDRM had opened an investigation paper following Sentul Police Report No. 4034/26, with the case being investigated under Section 124B of the Penal Code, which relates to activities detrimental to parliamentary democracy.
He said PDRM, through its International Relations Division and the Interpol Kuala Lumpur National Central Bureau (NCB), is working closely with its counterparts to determine the family’s exact whereabouts as part of efforts to facilitate their return to Malaysia.
“The government wishes to emphasise that all actions taken by PDRM are based on the rule of law, without prejudice or selective agendas, and are carried out to safeguard national security and uphold the integrity of the legal system,” he said.
On May 26, Bernama reported that Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar confirmed that Na’imah and her two sons, Muhammed Amir Zainuddin and Muhammed Amin Zainuddin, had been abroad since investigations into an alleged plot to topple the government began earlier this year.
He said police had recorded statements from 17 individuals to assist investigations under Section 124B of the Penal Code, and that the investigation paper had since been referred to the Attorney General’s Chambers for further action. — Bernama

Title: 4WD slips off deteriorating Baram logging bridge, renewing concerns over rural road safety
Article:
MIRI, July 3 — A near-tragic incident involving a four-wheel-drive (4WD) vehicle on a deteriorating logging bridge along the road to SK Long Pelutan has renewed concerns over poor road conditions in the Baram interior.
The incident occurred on Wednesday when the vehicle reportedly slipped off while crossing a damaged bridge along the route linking Long Lama and Miri, a road frequently used by teachers travelling to and from SK Long Pelutan.
No teachers or schoolchildren were in the vehicle at the time.
The vehicle belonged to the family of Salyndra Anyta, who shared details of the incident on social media.
“The road conditions at the scene are extremely poor and dangerous. My father’s vehicle slipped and almost plunged into the ravine,” she told
The Borneo Post
.
Salyndra, who hails from Long Tepen, said the bridge had been repaired by villagers just two days before the incident.
She said that although several logging companies operate in the area, the road remains poorly maintained, making it hazardous for motorists, especially during bad weather.
“There are several other sections that are even in worse condition,” she said, adding that the deteriorating road has long disrupted access to the area and endangered road users.
The vehicle was eventually pulled to safety without injuries or further incident.
According to Salyndra, similar incidents have occurred at the same location in the past, highlighting the need for urgent intervention.
“The same problem, the same location, the same risk,” she said.
She questioned how many more incidents must occur before remedial action is taken, stressing that her concern is not about assigning blame but ensuring accountability in maintaining rural infrastructure.
“The safety of road users should be a priority, not something that only receives attention after an accident has happened,” she said.
She urged the relevant authorities to carry out immediate repair works before a more serious tragedy occurs.
At the time of writing, no official statement had been issued by the relevant authorities regarding the incident or any planned repair works.
Confirmation has also been sought on the condition of the affected road and bridge. — The Borneo Post

Title: Ex‑kindergarten teacher charged with laundering RM133,550 linked to alleged child‑relocation scheme to New Zealand
Article:
SHAH ALAM, July 3 — A former kindergarten teacher pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to three counts of money laundering involving RM133,550 in cash and gold jewellery allegedly linked to a scheme promising to facilitate a child’s relocation to New Zealand last year.
A. Venmani, 50, entered the plea after the charges were read out before Judge Datin Fatimah Zahari.
According to the charges, Venmani is accused of receiving proceeds from unlawful activities in three separate transactions involving RM83,550, RM50,000, two gold chains and a gold pendant from a 39-year-old local woman.
The complainant had allegedly entrusted the accused with facilitating her child’s relocation to New Zealand for a better and more secure future.
The offences were allegedly committed at bank branches in Bandar Baru Rawang and Kota Kemuning, as well as at a jewellery shop in Sri Muda, Shah Alam, between January and February last year.
The charges were framed under Section 4(1)(b) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Act 613), punishable under Section 4(1) of the same Act.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of 15 years’ imprisonment and a fine of not less than five times the amount or value of the proceeds of the unlawful activity or RM5 million, whichever is higher, upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Wan Ahmad Hakim Wan Ahmad Jaafar proposed bail of RM30,000 for each charge, along with additional conditions requiring the accused to surrender her passport, report to the nearest police station once a month and refrain from interfering with prosecution witnesses.
Counsel Farith Emier Farhan Morni appealed for a lower bail, informing the court that his client had recently lost her job and was also supporting her ailing mother.
“The accused was previously charged in another court and granted bail totalling RM30,000. I have been informed that her savings have been exhausted and she now depends on financial assistance from family members,” he said.
The court allowed bail of RM15,000 with all the additional conditions requested by the prosecution and fixed Aug 10 for mention. — Bernama

Title: Anwar boosts taxi replacement fund by RM10m, says dedicated Proton S70 financing in the works
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced an additional RM10 million allocation for the Vehicle Replacement Matching Grant Programme to help taxi drivers replace ageing vehicles.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the additional funding follows the encouraging response to the initial RM10 million allocation for the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme, announced in Budget 2026.
He said the government has also reached an agreement with Proton and the Transport Ministry to develop a dedicated financing scheme enabling taxi drivers to own the Proton S70.
“The response to the allocation announced in the Budget has been very encouraging. Given the importance of the taxi industry, I have approved an immediate additional RM10 million specifically for taxi reform,” he said at the launch of the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme at Dataran Merdeka today.
Also present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
Meanwhile, the Prime Minister said the government remains committed to strengthening the taxi industry through improvements to the existing system to safeguard drivers’ welfare in line with technological change and the evolving transport landscape.
He said issues affecting the taxi sector are not only linked to drivers’ livelihoods but also to technological shifts that have left the industry behind, as well as the fact that many drivers are now older.
Anwar said the government had inherited a weak system, including the practice of issuing taxi permits to companies or individuals rather than directly to drivers.
“One of the issues raised over decades is why permits are not given directly to drivers so that they become owners and earn a good halal income,” he said.
The Transport Ministry, he noted, is currently reviewing various approaches to improve the system and help taxi drivers and the industry adapt to technological developments.
Anwar also urged local authorities to provide more comfortable holding areas for taxi drivers while waiting for or picking up passengers.
On the National MADANI Taxi Reform Programme, he expressed confidence that the initiative could increase drivers’ net income to as much as RM7,000 per month, taking into account savings from maintenance services and vehicle financing arrangements.
The programme features the Proton S70 as the designated taxi model, selected for its suitability in terms of safety, comfort and fuel efficiency.
The approach aligns with the government’s efforts to promote the use of modern, higher-quality taxis to improve public confidence in the service.
To support the programme, DRB-HICOM Group, through EON-Avis, together with financial institutions, is offering financing options to help taxi drivers transition to new vehicles based on their needs and affordability. — Bernama

Title: Armizan: Applications open today for diesel‑subsidy scheme for company jeeps, pick‑ups; payments start July 15
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Applications for the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) have opened today for the land transport sector covering company-owned private vehicles, including jeeps and pick-up trucks, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said.
He said successful applicants will become eligible for the diesel subsidy from July 15, subject to approval and the issuance of a fleet card.
“To qualify, vehicles must be registered as business vehicles under the Company Private Use (AE) class/code in the Road Transport Department’s (JPJ) MySikap system.
“In addition, the business must be registered as a sole proprietorship or partnership with the Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) or the relevant local authority in Sabah and Sarawak,” he said in a statement today.
Armizan said the subsidy is intended to benefit registered micro and small businesses operating as sole proprietorships and partnerships.
Eligible business owners are encouraged to submit their applications as soon as possible through the MySubsidi portal at https://mysubsidi.kpdn.gov.my.
The government had previously extended SKDS eligibility to two other categories: the public land transport sector and the consumer goods land transport sector. — Bernama

Title: PLUS: Smart Lane at Bandar Saujana Putra-Putra Heights to close for five months for maintenance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Smart Lane from Bandar Saujana Putra to Putra Heights (Northbound) at KM15.9 to KM13.9 of the North-South Expressway Central Link (ELITE) will be temporarily closed for approximately five months from Monday, July 6, until December 3.
PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) in a statement today said the temporary closure is to facilitate maintenance works as part of its ongoing efforts to improve the highway infrastructure and ensure the safety of highway users.
“Throughout the maintenance works, the existing three lanes at the affected stretch will remain open to maintain traffic flow and to minimise traffic disruptions for highway users,” the statement read.
PLUS also encouraged highway users to plan their journeys using the PLUS app for the latest traffic updates and obey all traffic signage and instructions issued by PLUS personnel when passing through the construction zone.
For traffic updates, users may also refer to the PUTRI Virtual Assistant, X@plustrafik, electronic message signboards (VMS) at selected locations or contact the PLUSLine at 1800-88-0000 in case of emergencies. — Bernama

Title: Executor of late Waz Lian co-founder Ta Kin Yan’s estate says will is clear, leaves no room for dispute
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The executor and trustee of the late Tan Sri Ta Kin Yan’s estate said the late businessman’s will is clear in its intent and leaves no room for dispute.
Datuk Poh Po Lian, who co-founded the Waz Lian Group with Ta in 1987, said Ta’s will — which had been kept in a bank safe — provides a clear and equitable framework for administering the estate.
The will, dated February 4, 2008, appoints Poh as the executor and trustee of Ta’s estate.
“So, I am aware of the family tension and must remain impartial. My foremost duty is to administer the distribution of assets.
“Nevertheless, I remain committed to honouring Tan Sri’s trust and carrying out his wishes.
“The will’s intent is clear and unambiguous, leaving no room for dispute,” he told
Malay Mail
in a brief interview recently.
On March 11, the High Court of Malaya granted probate for Ta’s estate to Poh, nearly two years after Ta’s death at age 72.
A probate is a legal document that allows the executor to manage and distribute a deceased person’s assets according to their will, ensuring the estate is handled properly.
Regarding the timeline, Poh said it was difficult for him to estimate how long the estate administration process will take because ongoing family disputes and legal proceedings have added considerable complexity.
Poh said he is currently working with asset distribution lawyers to identify Ta’s global assets, including both corporate and personal holdings, with Singapore-based law firm Allen & Gledhill appointed to facilitate the distribution of the estate to the beneficiaries.
“Trust and kindness were the foundation of his (Tan Sri’s) life and career, and he firmly believed in giving all his children equal opportunities.
“A forensic accounting review will also be conducted to ensure transparency in the estate’s assets and distributions,” he said.
Ta, who died in his sleep on June 15, 2024, was also founder of Majestic Gen Sdn Bhd and had business interests spanning hotels, resorts, manufacturing, and golf clubs across Malaysia, China, Macau, and Hong Kong.

Title: Foreign woman reported missing since June 30 found dead in Penampang apartment; police rule out foul play
Article:
PENAMPANG, July 3 — A 42-year-old foreign woman who had been reported missing since June 30 was found dead in her apartment along Jalan Penampang Lama.
Penampang police chief Supt Sammy Newton said the victim, who lived alone, was found by her employer who had become concerned when repeated attempts to contact her since that morning went unanswered.
“Upon arriving at the residence, the employer found the apartment door locked and received no response from the victim, and proceeded to contact the authorities.
“With assistance from the Fire and Rescue Department, police personnel forced open the door and found the victim dead inside,” he said in a statement.
He said based on initial investigations, the victim had informed her employer that she would be on leave on June 29 and return to work the next day.
Sammy added that the investigation also found no signs of foul play in connection with the woman’s demise.
He urged the public to refrain from speculating on the incident, and not to circulate photographs or videos out of respect for the victim’s family and next of kin.
The case has been classified as sudden death pending the results of a post-mortem. — The Borneo Post

Title: Anti-financial crime body says Azam Baki’s board role is in ‘individual capacity’, not tied to MACC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The National Financial Crime Prevention Centre (NFCC) today insisted that Tan Sri Azam Baki was appointed to its advisory board in his individual capacity, and not for his former position as MACC chief commissioner.
In a statement today, the NFCC also said the appointment was made with the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.
“Therefore, his membership in the NFCC advisory board is not tied to the position of chief commissioner of the MACC and remains in force until the appointment expires, unless terminated earlier by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong,” the NFCC said.
The NFCC also clarified that Azam’s term is valid for three years, running from September 2024 to September 2027.
This defence follows a challenge from opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, who questioned the justification for Azam’s continued involvement given that he is currently under investigation.
